description: Mattermost helps us a lot in communicating with the team. We can communicate with different teams and different projects. The tool has become essential for a good performance of projects.
author: Jander S.
pros: Communication with channels, private or public; Individual communication; Integration with other solutions: Gitlab, for example; Possibility of using plugins.
cons: Some bugs in notifications sometimes make us miss messages; Plugins do not work in the mobile app;

description: A slight learning curve, but in the end a very reliable and rebost system. Have gone through 3 major upgrades with no problems. I prefer a DIY approach to something like Teams under Microsoft control.
author: Mario G.
pros: Running on a VPS, the free version is super cheap for unlimited users. Supports all major client platforms including browser.
cons: Updates can be a probem, and sometimes user's options for 'auto update check' and 'system tray color' seem to get lost.

review_title: Mattermost, open-source but not that great
author: Edgar H.
pros: Open-source, easily installable. Quite fast.
cons: Not so great working with threads, chats are messed up. Some competitors do much better in this regard (zulip)

review_title: COMMUNICATE EASILY WITH MATTERMOST... Highly recommend
description: Ive used mattermost for 2.4 years now and it was a great experience The customer care service is legit

pros: It has helped me to connect with my colleagues It is super easy to use It is pocket friendly Mattermost saves me alot of time since i can send large files at once
cons: Mattermost has helped me connect with my colleagues and clients seamlessly No complaints at all i highly recommend

author: Saad U.
pros: Mattermost offers robust business instant messaging and live chat capabilities which allows us (teams) to seamlessly collaborate in real-time.
cons: Unavailability of builtin video conferencing capability is the only flaw but I have integrated with external web conferencing applications.

description: Ever since i started using mattermost it has been a great experience The customer care service are so friendly
author: Dr.Enoch E.
pros: I love the fact that i can coordinate with my colleagues It is super easy to use It is cost effective It has an intuitive interface
cons: Mattermost helps you to collaborate with your colleagues seamlessly No dislike at all

author: Linet G.
pros: Mattermost allows me to connect with colleagues and customers via audio calls. Sending large files is another great aspect about this tool.
cons: There is nothing which I dislike about this program at the moment.

review_title: Mattermost: Full-featured, Iconic and Robustious BIM Program
description: Large file sharing and team collaboration get enhanced by this tool.
author: Zandalee Z.
pros: Mattermost streamlines business communication in one place via creation of unlimited communication channels.

author: Austine O.
pros: Mattermost unify teams in one place through unlimited communication channels.

author: Kioko M.
pros: Mattermost unify teams in one place through unlimited communication channels in one place.

author: Esther M.
pros: Mattermost gives me the ability to coordinate on projects with colleagues via company communication channels.

review_title: Internal Communication Matters Most in Every Organization
author: Ewa B.
pros: Mattermost has a mobile application which I use on a daily basis to send files and collaborate vi a instant messaging.
cons: To conference through video calls it requires to be integrated with external apps.

review_title: Instant Messaging and Live Chat Platform
description: Team management, internal communication and bug tracking are at par with this tool always.
author: Bennett W.
pros: Mattermost boost internal communication via creation of unlimited communications channels in one single repository.
cons: I have been using Mattermost for an extended duration now and I rarely encounter challenges whenever I turn to it.

description: Screen sharing enhances large file sharing via drag and drop.Business instant messaging.
author: Emily M.
pros: I like how Mattermost has a lot of features:- internal communication, screen sharing, web conferencing, business instant messaging and chat all in one place.
cons: For nearly a decade now Mattermost has been a useful program, with no drawbacks in business matters.

review_title: Mattermost Matters Most in Our Agency
author: Jack H.
pros: Mattermost boost productivity by unifying teams in my organization in one single workspace through corporate communication channels.
cons: Video conferencing isn’t robust enough that’s why it demands to be integrated with external web conferencing systems like Zoom Meetings.

review_title: Mattermost for Real-time BIM and PM
description: Project management and business instant messaging.
author: Bradley B.
pros: Mattermost combines messaging and project management capabilities in one place.It has a robust backend integration feature, which boost it’s capabilities.Project coordination is another great thing about this tool.
cons: I do not like that Mattermost demands to be integrated with external web conferencing apps to boost video conferencing.

review_title: All we need for successful communication
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use and very user friendly UI. You can edit messages even after sending. Tag people whom you want the message to be seen in a channel. Easily accessible channels. It's a great open source application.
cons: Screen sharing, calling and video conferencing option is not available which makes us dependent on other apps. Last seen online is not displayed which should be available.

review_title: Switched to Mattermost after using Slack extensively, but got sick of the 10000 message limit
description: A platform to discuss issues and feature ideas around our open source project, without cluttering up the threads on github. A great way to stay in touch with devs from all around the world.
author: Adam W.
pros: That we can host it ourselves, for one. We used to use the free tier of slack, but quickly discovered that the message limit was causing issues when we needed to recall conversations had in the past. It's been relatively hassle-free in getting it set up, and the whole team love it! One or two of us (myself included) were inspired to brush up on some of our coding skills when we discovered it didn't quite have the integrations that slack did, and as such have learned a lot about how Mattermost is put together, and how to create bots to get the information we need via webhooks. The Mattermost developers and community at large have been very helpful in answering questions when we've come up against issues, and I have even got a couple of my integrations featured on the integrations page of their website!
cons: Really, not a whole lot. Ideally I would like to be able to sign into multiple slack servers from the mobile app (though, the native app has really come along in leaps and bounds recently). The only thing I miss from the Slack days is a sidebar/widget that tells you the local times of the other users (handy when working with folk from overseas!), though we have recently developed our own slash command to return this information, it would be nice if it was built in to the server. I should probably feed this back to the devs.... ;)

description: We ended up looking for an alternative to Mattermost because of Slack's price-performance ratio and would switch again today.
author: Silvan M.
pros: Mattermost is constantly being developed and you can tell that it's going further. In addition, as a collaboration tool, it provides the most important information. No more and no less.
cons: At the beginning (approx. 2018) it was still a bit unstable and some important features were still missing. This has gotten much better in 2022. Over the years, with many users, maintenance and upkeep should never be neglected. Otherwise it will be slow and have long loading times when changing channels etc. Plugins work, but not all are mature (e.g. Jitsi caused problems for us)

review_title: Mostly good, but I do feel it is a slightly lesser experience than some proprietary options.
description: Control over our hosting and data
author: Shawn M.
pros: Open source and self hosted, I don't get irritated if slack goes down and I don't have to worry about my data security or my data just being lost of the company goes belly up. This is far and away our primary reason for using this instead of Slack,Hipchat,Cisco, etc. It doesn't ask anywhere on this survey, but we use it for about 80 people, 60 staff and then various clients and contractors. We don't use many of the enterprise features, we just pay the licensing to support open source.
cons: iOS and Android Apps are pretty idiocy, they're getting a bit better, but they are easily the worst part and I field lots of complaints about them. I would trade all new features and other fixes for faster and more reliable apps. The web interface is much better, but a bit buggy, nothing serious but little lags and quirks persist longer than I would expect. The install process is also a bit silly, copy pasting files and such, a provided .deb or a custom apt repository would be 100x better and save me upgrade time and downtime. The lack of this is surprising considering almost all other software I use has this, even when it is free or small.

description: Apart from the shortcut breaking the @-mentioning on my AZERTY keyboard, mattermost is a fantastic tool.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The ability to add many channels, with/without passwords, as and when needed. Combining multiple servers with separate usersets.
cons: Since one of the more recent versions, they've altered a couple of shortcuts. One of these means I can no longer type an @-sign, making mentioning people rather difficult.

review_title: Very intuitive and solid software which is perfect for running a slack replacement on premise.
description: A super and ready to use Slack alternative.
author: Tobias F.
pros: * The replacement of slack * Mattermost has almost the same features as Slack * You can host and run your own instance on your own server (For companies which hate clouds) :) * You have a good overview of logs and you can see what goes around * The statistics gives a good point when a dev team is productive * You can easily create unlimited integrations which some internal stuff
cons: * The lack of integrations (Slack is here over the top) * The incoming hook limitation of maximum content length is the hell (Mattermost don't inform the customer about a message which was higher above the hard coded message size limit) * UX in Slack is a little bit better (Slack is massively using websockets and eventstreams to fetch the actual team messages) -> You see always whats going on * A 'Unread' channel (like in slack) would be a pleasure ;) * A step by step guide for creating an integration would not bad for not advanced users

description: Very good. Prefer it to slack as it can be self hosted
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Self Hosted, easy to use and implement on our own servers.
cons: Needs Manual intervention to clean up old files

description: I use mattermos mohr than one year, but still can not used to it. main reason is that when I get the new notification, I don't get some signal, or beep. it doesn't appears on screen. mattermost is in my company main communication system. if I would asked, I would changed it with skype for business, or lync
author: paata E.
pros: mattermost is our companies inside network. we use it everyday to communicate. I like it because I have it also in my mobile. now when I work remotely, i am not always nearby computer and with mattermost I am always in touch.
cons: I use mattermos mohr than one year, but still can not used to it. main reason is that when I get the new notification, I don't get some signal, or beep. it doesn't appears on screen.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - Mattermost is intuitive to use and easy to implement in your company - It has a good amount of integrations
cons: - Not as many options for building bots, automations and intgrations as competitors - Mobile app only "okay" - Most other organizations are on Slack, so if using a tool across companies is an important use case, Slack is the better option
